commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR clamps out-of-range WhatsApp Apple-epoch timestamps to zero during import and on stored timestamp reads, with regression coverage for the `0@status` chat sentinel and already-populated chat archives.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main converts impossible positive timestamps into year 11001 values that `time.Time.MarshalJSON` cannot encode, and the PR body includes real terminal proof for the chat/unread failure. I did not run tests because this review must keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update `Store.Status` to use the shared clamp and add a status regression for a poisoned message timestamp.

target.title: fix(whatsappdb): clamp impossible Apple-epoch timestamps so --json survives 0@status
target.body:
## Problem

`wacrawl --json chats` / `unread` / `status` fail with:

```
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
```

and emit empty stdout + exit 1. The human (table) output is unaffected, so it only surfaces under `--json`.

## Cause

WhatsApp Desktop stores a sentinel `ZLASTMESSAGEDATE` for pseudo-chats such as the `0@status` broadcast chat. `appleTime()` interprets it as a literal Apple-epoch value, which converts to an impossible year (~11001). That value lands in the archive, and `encoding/json` then can't marshal it (`time.Time.MarshalJSON` rejects years outside `[0,9999]`) for any command whose JSON output includes the row — `chats`/`unread` directly, `status` via `max(ts)`. It also makes the pseudo-chat sort to the top of chat listings.

## Fix

Two symmetric clamps, one at each boundary the timestamp crosses:

1. **Import** (`appleNullTime`, `internal/whatsappdb/desktop.go`) — the single function every imported timestamp flows through. Impossible Apple-epoch conversions normalize to the zero value, so the sentinel never enters the archive (and sorts as oldest rather than newest). Fixes fresh imports at the root.
2. **Read** (`fromUnix`, `internal/store/store.go`) — the single function every stored timestamp flows through on read. An out-of-range persisted value (from an archive populated by a pre-fix build) clamps to zero, so an **already-populated archive self-heals without a forced re-import** — `--sync never` reads stop failing immediately. Mirrors the import clamp exactly.

No public JSON model types change.

## Test

- `TestAppleNullTimeNormalizesSentinel` — a sentinel converts to zero, a valid timestamp still converts correctly, invalid/non-positive input stays zero.
- `TestListChatsClampsOutOfRangePersistedTimestamp` — an archive whose stored `last_message_at` is out of range (simulating a pre-fix import) survives `ListChats`/`ListUnreadChats` + `json.Marshal`.

`go test ./...` passes; `go vet ./...` clean.

---

## Real-behavior proof (redacted)

macOS, real WhatsApp Desktop archive. Both sides built from source. `--sync never` reads the stored snapshot deterministically (no re-import); `--sync always` forces a fresh import. Private paths/content redacted.

```console
# 1) Reproduce on origin/main @ 7c4f382 (import re-stores the 0@status sentinel):
$ wacrawl --sync never --json chats ; echo "exit=$?"
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
exit=1
$ wacrawl --sync never --json unread ; echo "exit=$?"
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
exit=1

#    stored sentinel row (year ~11001):
$ sqlite3 ~/.wacrawl/wacrawl.db 'select count(*),sum(last_message_at>253402300799),max(last_message_at) from chats'
176 chats | 1 out-of-range | max=284990875200

# 2) Fix branch — read-side clamp: already-populated archive succeeds WITHOUT re-import (--sync never):
$ wacrawl --sync never --json status ; echo "exit=$?"   # valid JSON
exit=0
$ wacrawl --sync never --json chats | jq 'length'   # valid JSON, 50 records
exit=0
$ wacrawl --sync never --json unread | jq 'length'  # valid JSON, 26 records
exit=0

#    the out-of-range row is STILL in the store — reads are clamped, no re-import needed:
$ sqlite3 ~/.wacrawl/wacrawl.db 'select count(*),sum(last_message_at>253402300799) from chats'
176 chats | 1 out-of-range

# 3) Import-side clamp also keeps it out on a fresh import (--sync always), so it never re-enters:
$ wacrawl --sync always --json chats | jq 'length'  # valid JSON, exit 0
$ sqlite3 ~/.wacrawl/wacrawl.db 'select sum(last_message_at>253402300799) from chats'
0
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: rmorgans

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: rmorgans
commands.command: re-review — addressed the p2 (already-populated archives). added a symmetric read-side clamp in `store.fromunix` plus `testlistchatsclampsoutofrangepersistedtimestamp`; an out-of-range persisted timestamp now clamps on read, so a pre-fix archive survives `--json` under `--sync never` with no forced re-import (proof updated in the pr body). commit ef692fc
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressed the P2 (already-populated archives). Added a symmetric read-side clamp in `store.fromUnix` plus `TestListChatsClampsOutOfRangePersistedTimestamp`; an out-of-range persisted timestamp now clamps on read, so a pre-fix archive survives `--json` under `--sync never` with no forced re-import (proof updated in the PR body). Commit ef692fc.
